title: Thunderstorm, heatwave conditions likely across Odisha over next 5 days - Odisha TV
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
full_text:
Odisha is set to experience a mixed spell of thunderstorms, gusty winds, heatwave conditions, and high humidity over the next five days, according to the latest weather forecast.
Light to moderate rain or thunderstorms are likely to occur in several districts, particularly across southern, coastal, and northern regions of the state.
On April 22, rainfall activity is expected in districts such as Kalahandi, Kandhamal, Rayagada, Koraput, Angul, Mayurbhanj, Balasore, Cuttack, Puri, Khordha, Ganjam, and Gajapati, while other areas may witness dry weather.
The pattern is likely to continue over the next two days, with districts including Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Puri, Khurda, Ganjam, Rayagada, and Koraput remaining vulnerable to thunderstorms and light rainfall. However, by April 24 and 25, rainfall activity is expected to reduce and remain largely confined to southern districts such as Gajapati, Rayagada, Koraput, Malkangiri, and Kandhamal, with dry weather prevailing elsewhere.
The Meteorological Department has issued a Yellow Warning for several districts, cautioning about thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds, particularly during the afternoon and evening hours.
Wind speeds may reach up to 40–50 kmph in districts like Keonjhar and Mayurbhanj initially, while other affected regions may experience winds of 30–40 kmph. Despite the possibility of rain, heatwave conditions are expected to intensify in parts of western and central Odisha from April 23 onwards, particularly in Jharsuguda, Balangir, Angul, and Kandhamal.

In addition, warm night conditions are likely in districts such as Kalahandi, Sundargarh, Sambalpur, and Bolangir, offering little respite from the heat even after sunset. Coastal districts, including Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khordha, and Ganjam, are expected to witness hot and humid conditions, increasing overall discomfort levels.
Overall, the state is likely to face a combination of localized thunderstorms and rising temperatures, reflecting a typical pre-monsoon weather pattern. Residents have been advised to remain cautious during lightning and gusty winds, avoid unnecessary outdoor exposure during peak heat hours, and stay hydrated as Odisha continues to experience fluctuating weather conditions.

title: Odisha Sizzles Under Blazing Sun, No Relief till April 25 - pragativadi.com
full_text:
Odisha continues to reel under intense heat as temperatures crossed 40°C across 18 cities on Tuesday.
Jharsuguda emerged as the hottest city at 43°C, followed by Titilagarh at 42°C.
The India Meteorological Department reported that western Odisha districts, including Balangir, Jharsuguda, and Angul, will face severe heatwave conditions until April 25. The department also issued a yellow warning for 11 districts due to extreme heat and humidity.
The coastal belt experienced sweltering conditions, with humidity adding to the discomfort. Meteorologists predicted no relief from the heat until April 25. They expect temperatures to remain steady for the next four days before rising by another 2°C to 3°C.
Jharsuguda, Titilagarh, and Sambalpur recorded scorching temperatures, while other regions such as Sundargarh, Sonepur, and Bhawanipatna also hovered around 41°C. The IMD forecast indicated that the heatwave will persist, with western Odisha continuing to burn under the blazing sun.
Residents have been advised to stay indoors during peak hours and remain hydrated. The prolonged heatwave has disrupted daily life, with schools adjusting schedules and outdoor activities curtailed.
The state braces for more intense heat as the mercury shows no sign of dropping.

title: Western Odisha to Sizzle at 45°C, IMD Issues Thunderstorm Alert - pragativadi.com
full_text:
Western Odisha braces for extreme heat as temperatures may soar to 45°C in several areas over the next five days.
Jharsuguda, Titilagarh, and Balangir are expected to record the highest temperatures, while other western districts may experience heat above 42°C.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) confirmed that western Odisha will continue to face intense heat conditions. Coastal and southern regions will remain comparatively cooler but humid. Residents across Titilagarh, Balangir, and Jharsuguda reported scorching afternoons and rising discomfort levels.
Meanwhile, the IMD issued a thunderstorm and lightning warning for Koraput, Malkangiri, Gajapati, Rayagada, and Ganjam districts. These areas may witness light rainfall accompanied by gusty winds under the influence of the Kalbaishakhi system.
On Monday, 14 cities across Odisha recorded temperatures above 40°C. Titilagarh registered 42.5°C, Jharsuguda 42.4°C, and Bhawanipatna, Balangir, and Angul crossed 41°C. Meteorologists advised people to avoid outdoor activities during peak hours and stay hydrated.
